message:
chore(release): bump version to 0.3.0

## Overview

This release introduces significant new features for context reduction
and local deployment scenarios, along with critical bug fixes for
stability and protocol correctness.

## New Features

### Stdio Transport Support (`serve-stdio` command)
- New CLI command `am serve-stdio` for running MCP server over stdio
- Enables project-local installation patterns without requiring HTTP server
- Suitable for direct integration with Claude Code and similar tools
- All logging properly redirected to stderr to preserve protocol integrity

### Tool Filtering for Context Reduction
- New `TOOLS_FILTER_ENABLED` and `TOOLS_FILTER_PROFILE` settings
- Predefined profiles: full, core, minimal, messaging
- Custom filtering via explicit include/exclude lists
- Reduces context overhead by up to ~70% for minimal workflows

### Push Notification Signals
- New `NOTIFICATIONS_ENABLED` setting for local deployments
- Signal files enable instant notification via inotify/FSEvents/kqueue
- Eliminates need for polling in local agent coordination
- Configurable debounce and metadata inclusion

## Bug Fixes

### Stdio Protocol Corruption Prevention
- Fixed: Rich console output (LLM cost logging) was writing to stdout
- Fixed: Tool debug panels were corrupting stdio protocol
- Both `TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED` and `LOG_RICH_ENABLED` now disabled for stdio

### Resilience and Stability
- EMFILE (too many open files) recovery with automatic cache cleanup
- Python 3.14 cancellation safety in async session management
- SQLite pool tuning for better concurrent access
- Fixed project handling edge cases

### Other Fixes
- Excluded HTML from viewer SRI map to fix integrity checks
- Fixed flaky tests for CI reliability
- Proper type casts for SQLAlchemy where clauses

## Testing

- Comprehensive test coverage for tool filtering and notifications
- E2E test updates for advisory file reservation model
- Global resource cleanup fixture for FD leak prevention

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 description = "Coordinated multi-agent messaging and coordination MCP server."
 readme = "README.md"
 requires-python = ">=3.14"

     { name = "aiolimiter" },
